text.title: Rescue Mechanics, Measurement Rules, and Antipatterns
text.summary: At-risk signals, rescue interventions, trial extension policy, measurement window rules, and antipatterns that silently destroy conversion.

section.title: At-Risk Signals and Rescue

rule.statement: Flag trials as at-risk using behavioral signals — not time elapsed. Four key flags: no login in 3+ days, onboarding not completed, only 1 feature used, no team invites in a team product.
rule.rationale: Time-based rescue emails ("you haven't logged in") fire on users who are active in-app; signal-based rescue fires only when behavior confirms disengagement.

rule.statement: Offer trial extensions only to genuinely inactive trials with a credible stated reason. Do not offer extensions broadly — broad extensions train users to procrastinate conversion.
rule.rationale: Inactive trials with a genuine blocker accept extensions at 30-50% rate. Offering extensions to active users who simply haven't converted yet defers conversion without adding value.

list.item: No login signal → "Did you get stuck?" email with one specific offer of help.
Stuck in onboarding → Offer 1:1 setup call or video walkthrough.
Low feature usage → Feature education email with a concrete use-case example.
Churned without feedback → Exit survey with single question and free-text field.

section.reference: Python function to classify trial at-risk flags from behavior data

rule.statement: Track trial-to-paid conversion at Day 14, Day 30, and Day 60. Deferred converters (Day 21-60) are real and typically account for 20-30% of total conversions from a cohort.
rule.rationale: Measuring only at trial-end (Day 14) underestimates the true trial-to-paid rate and leads to over-investment in urgency tactics while under-investing in deferred nurture.

rule.statement: Expose pricing during the trial — do not hide it. Hidden pricing breaks trust and lowers conversion. Users should be able to see and compare plans from Day 1.
rule.rationale: Transparency about pricing during trial is a PLG principle; surfacing it only at the end creates decision paralysis at the worst moment — the trial-end deadline.

antipattern.description: Email sequence with no stop-on-activation rule that keeps emailing active users through the entire 14-day sequence.
antipattern.reason: Active users receiving "you haven't tried feature X" emails churn from the email list, reducing the channel's future effectiveness.

antipattern.description: Behavioral trigger logic using OR conditions where AND is required — e.g., "no login OR incomplete onboarding" fires on everyone including users who logged in but skipped one optional step.
antipattern.reason: Over-triggering rescue emails trains users to ignore them; false positives waste human CS effort and dilute the rescue signal.

antipattern.description: Activation metric chosen by product team intuition without cohort validation.
antipattern.reason: Unvalidated metrics optimize for the wrong behavior. Optimizing "invited a teammate" when team invites do not actually predict conversion wastes all downstream effort.

antipattern.description: Hard paywall at trial end that requires data export before access is cut.
antipattern.reason: Data-loss threats trigger negative reviews and word-of-mouth damage. Offering a read-only grace period preserves goodwill and dramatically improves win-back rate.
